review_scope: Post-implementation code review for TaskCompleted hook validation

observations.note: Hook script implements secure JSON validation using jq --arg/--argjson, preventing command/jq injection
Comprehensive test coverage: 29 tests across 6 ACs, all passing, with edge cases and error conditions
Graceful error handling implements BR-001 correctly: errors exit 0, only proven violations exit 2
Robust project root detection uses CLAUDE.md marker file with walk-up tree algorithm, prevents path traversal
settings.json properly registers TaskCompleted hook with correct timeout (15s) and path interpolation
Logging uses inconsistent format (DEBUG:, WARN:, BLOCK:) without ISO8601 timestamps required by spec NFR-001
Consider adding explicit step_id format validation (must match ^[0-9]+(\.[0-9]+)*$) for extra robustness
Document expected registry JSON schema in header comment for maintainability
OR-logic implementation correctly uses jq --argjson with index() for safe array matching
Test anti-gaming validation: no skip decorators, empty tests, TODOs, or excessive mocking detected
All variable usage properly quoted, preventing word splitting and globbing vulnerabilities
Performance excellent: hook executes well under 500ms NFR requirement, no bottlenecks

rationale: Hook implementation meets all acceptance criteria with zero security vulnerabilities. Comprehensive test coverage (29/29 passing) and robust error handling. Single medium-priority issue (logging format) does not block integration.

recommendations.action: Implement ISO8601 timestamp logging to meet spec NFR-001 requirement
Add explicit step_id format validation regex check
recommendations.impact: defensive programming, catches malformed subjects early

recommendations.action: Document registry JSON schema in script header
